summary: The heavy path conjecture from Conjecture (informal) in arXiv:1904.12273 — that when one vertex of a C-major set M is nonadjacent to all others, a single path of C of bounded length catches every vertex of M — was explicitly left unresolved by the authors, who replaced it with a more complicated argument. A targeted web search across 2020–2026 found no follow-up paper proving or disproving this specific statement. Given the conjecture is ~6 years old and the search returned nothing, absence of published progress is plausible but not certain for such a technical algorithmic lemma.
search_queries: Chudnovsky Scott Seymour "long odd hole" "heavy path" conjecture graph theory
arxiv 1904.12273 "detecting a long odd hole" follow-up citations
Chudnovsky Scott Seymour "C-major" "long odd hole" "bounded length" path 2021 2022 2023 2024
"long odd hole" "heavy path" OR "C-major" graph algorithm Chudnovsky Seymour 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up paper specifically addressing the heavy path extension was found after 5 web calls. The statement is informal (not labelled as a formal conjecture in the paper) and concerns a structural lemma internal to an algorithmic proof; progress may exist in unpublished notes or in successor papers not indexed by the queries used. Related follow-up work by the same group (arXiv:2004.11874 on shortest odd hole, arXiv:2009.05691 on long even holes) was identified but not verified to address this specific conjecture.
